Let $O P=\gamma$; the angle between $O Q$ and the positive $x$-axis be $\theta$; and the angle between $O P$ and the positive $z$-axis be $\phi$, where $O$ is the origin. Then the distance of $P$ from the $x$-axis is

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: Let the line $\mathrm{L}$ intersect the lines $x-2=-y=z-1,2(x+1)=2(y-1)=z+1$ and be parallel to the line $\frac{x-2}{3}=\frac{y-1}{1}=\frac{z-2}{2}$. Then which of the following points lies on L?
questions.solution:
$\mathrm{L}_1: \frac{\mathrm{x}-2}{1}=\frac{\mathrm{y}}{-1}=\frac{\mathrm{z}-1}{1}=\lambda$ $\mathrm{L}_2: \frac{\mathrm{x}+1}{\frac{1}{2}}=\frac{\mathrm{y}-1}{\frac{1}{2}}=\frac{\mathrm{z}+1}{1}=\mu$ dr of line MN will be $ < 3+\lambda-\frac{\mu}{2},-1-\lambda-\frac{\mu}{2}, 2+\lambda-\mu>\&$ it will be proportional to $ < 3,1,2>$
$\Rightarrow \lambda=-\frac{4}{3} \& \mu=-\frac{2}{3}$ $\therefore$ Coordinate of $\mathrm{M}$ will be $ < \left(\frac{2}{3}, \frac{4}{3},-\frac{1}{3}\right)$ and equation of required line will be. $\frac{\mathrm{x}-\frac{2}{3}}{3}=\frac{\mathrm{y}-\frac{4}{3}}{1}=\frac{\mathrm{z}+\frac{1}{3}}{2}=\mathrm{k}$ So any point on this line will be $\begin{aligned} & \left(\frac{2}{3}+3 \mathrm{k}, \frac{4}{3}+\mathrm{k},-\frac{1}{3}+2 \mathrm{k}\right) \\ & \because \frac{2}{3}+3 \mathrm{k}=-\frac{1}{3} \Rightarrow \mathrm{k}=-\frac{1}{3} \end{aligned}$ $\therefore$ Point lie on the line for $\mathrm{k}=-\frac{1}{3} \text { is }\left(-\frac{1}{3}, 1,-1\right)$
questions.source: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: If the mirror image of the point <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>P</mi><mo>(</mo><mn>3</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>4</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>9</mn><mo>)</mo></math> in the line <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo>−</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow><mn>3</mn></mfrac><mo>=</mo><mfrac><mrow><mi>y</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow><mn>2</mn></mfrac><mo>=</mo><mfrac><mrow><mi>z</mi><mo>−</mo><mn>2</mn></mrow><mn>1</mn></mfrac></math> is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mrow><mi>α</mi><mo>,</mo><mi>β</mi><mo>,</mo><mi>γ</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>,</mo></math> then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>14</mn><mfenced><mrow><mi>α</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>β</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>γ</mi></mrow></mfenced></math> is:
questions.solution:
The given point is P(3,4,9).

Let, Qα,β,γ be the image of P(3,4,9) and 'O' be the mid-point of PQ which is on the given line 

x-13=y+12=z-21=λ (say)

⇒x=3λ+1, y=2λ-1, z=λ+2 

let 3λ+1, 2λ-1, λ+2 be the co-ordinates of O.

Direction ratios of the line is a1=3, b1=2, c1=1

Also, direction ratios of PO are

3λ-2, 2λ-5, λ-7

As, PO is perpendicular to the line

33λ-2+22λ-5+λ-7=0

⇒14λ-23=0

⇒λ=2314

∴ O point is 3·2314+1, 2·2314-1, 2314+2

Since, O is mid-point of PQ

∴3·2314+1, 2·2314-1, 2314+2=3+α2,4+β2,9+γ2

So, on comparing both side and solving we get,

α=627, β=47, γ=-127

Hence, 14α+ β+γ=108
questions.source: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024

questions.q: Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>L</mi><mn>1</mn></msub><mo>:</mo><mover><mi>r</mi><mo>→</mo></mover><mo>=</mo><mfenced><mrow><mover><mi>i</mi><mo>^</mo></mover><mo>-</mo><mover><mi>j</mi><mo>^</mo></mover><mo>+</mo><mn>2</mn><mover><mi>k</mi><mo>^</mo></mover></mrow></mfenced><mo>+</mo><mi>λ</mi><mfenced><mrow><mover><mi>i</mi><mo>^</mo></mover><mo>-</mo><mover><mi>j</mi><mo>^</mo></mover><mo>+</mo><mn>2</mn><mover><mi>k</mi><mo>^</mo></mover></mrow></mfenced><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi>λ</mi><mo>∈</mo><mi>R</mi></math>, <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>L</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><mo>:</mo><mover><mi>r</mi><mo>→</mo></mover><mo>=</mo><mfenced><mrow><mover><mi>j</mi><mo>^</mo></mover><mo>-</mo><mover><mi>k</mi><mo>^</mo></mover></mrow></mfenced><mo>+</mo><mi>μ</mi><mfenced><mrow><mn>3</mn><mover><mi>i</mi><mo>^</mo></mover><mo>+</mo><mover><mi>j</mi><mo>^</mo></mover><mo>+</mo><mi>p</mi><mover><mi>k</mi><mo>^</mo></mover></mrow></mfenced><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi>μ</mi><mo>∈</mo><mi>R</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>L</mi><mn>3</mn></msub><mo>:</mo><mover><mi>r</mi><mo>→</mo></mover><mo>=</mo><mi>δ</mi><mo>(</mo><mi>l</mi><mover><mi>i</mi><mo>^</mo></mover><mo>+</mo><mi>m</mi><mover><mi>j</mi><mo>^</mo></mover><mo>+</mo><mi>n</mi><mover><mi>k</mi><mo>^</mo></mover><mo>)</mo><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi>δ</mi><mo>∈</mo><mi>R</mi></math> be three lines such that <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>L</mi><mn>1</mn></msub></math> is perpendicular to <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>L</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>L</mi><mn>3</mn></msub></math> is perpendicular to both <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>L</mi><mn>1</mn></msub></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>L</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math>. Then the point which lies on <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>L</mi><mn>3</mn></msub></math> is
questions.solution:
Given: L1⊥L2

⇒i^-j^+2k^.3i^+j^+pk^=0

⇒3-1+2p=0

⇒p=-1

Also, L3⊥L1, L2

So, L3∥L1×L2

⇒L1×L2=i^j^k^1-1231-1

⇒L1×L2=-i^+7j^+4k^

On comparing with L3:r→=δ(li^+mj^+nk^), we get that (-δ,7δ,4δ) will lie on L3.

Now, for δ=1 the point will be (-1,7,4).
questions.source: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: Let the line of the shortest distance between the lines L1:r→=i^+2j^+3k^+λi^−j^+k^ and L2:r→=4i^+5j^+6k^+μi^+j^−k^ intersect L1 and L2 at P and Q respectively. If α,β,γ is the midpoint of the line segment PQ, then 2α+β+γ is equal to___________
questions.solution:
Let point on L1 be P1+λi^+2-λj^+3+λk^ and point on L2 be Q4+μi^+5+μj^+6-μk^.

⇒PQ→=λ-μ-3i^+-λ-μ-3j^+λ+μ-3k^

Now, PQ→ ⊥ L1, L2

⇒λ-μ-3+λ+μ+3+λ+μ-3=0

⇒3λ+μ=3   ...i

Also, λ-μ-3+-λ-μ-3+-λ-μ+3=0

⇒-λ-3μ=3   ...ii

Now, solving above equations we get,

⇒3λ+μ-3λ-9μ=3+9

⇒-8μ=12

⇒μ=-32

⇒λ=32

⇒P≡52,12,92, Q≡52,72,152

So, mid-point of PQ is 52,2,6

⇒2α+β+γ=252+2+6

⇒2α+β+γ=21
questions.source: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>P</mi><mo> </mo><mtext>and</mtext><mo> </mo><mi>Q</mi></math> be the points on the line <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>3</mn></mrow><mn>8</mn></mfrac><mo>=</mo><mfrac><mrow><mi>y</mi><mo>−</mo><mn>4</mn></mrow><mn>2</mn></mfrac><mo>=</mo><mfrac><mrow><mi>z</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow><mn>2</mn></mfrac></math> which are at a distance of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>6</mn></math> units from the point <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>R</mi><mo>(</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>2</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>3</mn><mo>)</mo></math>. If the centroid of the triangle <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>P</mi><mi>Q</mi><mi>R</mi></math> is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mrow><mi>α</mi><mo>,</mo><mi>β</mi><mo>,</mo><mi>γ</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>,</mo></math> then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>α</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><msup><mi>β</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><msup><mi>γ</mi><mn>2</mn></msup></math> is:
questions.solution:
Let, x+38=y−42=z+12=λ.

⇒x=8λ-3, y=2λ+4, z=2λ-1

Let, P≡8λ-3, 2λ+4, 2λ-1.

Now, given R1,2,3 and PR=6 & QR=6.

So, by distance formula we get,

⇒8λ-42+2λ+22+2λ-42=36

⇒64λ2+16-64λ+4λ2+4+8λ+4λ2+16-16λ=36

⇒72λ2-72λ=0

⇒λ=0, 1

⇒P≡-3,4,-1 and Q≡5,6,1

Hence, centroid of △PQR will be,

α,β,γ≡1,4,1.

⇒α2+β2+γ2=18
questions.source: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: Let Q and R be the feet of perpendiculars from the point Pa, a, a on the lines x=y, z=1 and x=−y, z=−1 respectively. If ∠QPR is a right angle, then 12a2 is equal to ________
questions.solution:
Given,

Equation of line x=y, z=1

x1=y1=z−10=r

Now, let any point on the above line be Qr, r, 1

Similarly, for line x=−y, z=−1 we get,

x1=y−1=z+10=k

So, any point on the above line will be Rk, −k, −1

Now, the direction ratio of PQ→=a−ri^+a−rj^+a−1k^

Now, finding foot of perpendicular from P on line x1=y1=z−10 using perpendicular condition we get,

a-r+a−r+0=0

⇒2a=2r

⇒a=r

Hence, point Qa,a,1

And similarly, the direction ratio of 

PR→=a−ki+a+kj^+a+1k^

And again, finding foot of perpendicular from P on line x1=y−1=z+10 using perpendicular condition we get,

a−k−a−k=0⇒k=0

Hence, point R0,0,-1

Also, given PQ→⊥PR→

Now, using perpendicular condition we get,

a−ra−k+a−ra+k+a−1a+1=0

⇒a−aa−0+a−aa+0+a−1a+1=0

⇒a−1a+1=0

⇒a=1 or -1

Hence, 12a2=12
questions.source: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: Let $\mathrm{ABC}$ be a triangle of area $15 \sqrt{2}$ and the vectors $\overrightarrow{\mathrm{AB}}=\hat{i}+2 \hat{j}-7 \hat{k}, \overrightarrow{\mathrm{BC}}=\mathrm{a} \hat{i}+\mathrm{b} \hat{j}+\mathrm{ck}$ and $\overrightarrow{\mathrm{AC}}=6 \hat{i}+\mathrm{d} \hat{j}-2 \hat{k}, \mathrm{~d}>0$. Then the square of the length of the largest side of the triangle $\mathrm{ABC}$ is _______
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} & \text { Area }=\frac{1}{2}\left|\begin{array}{ccc} \hat{i} & \hat{j} & \hat{k} \\ 1 & 2 & -7 \\ 6 & d & -2 \end{array}\right|=15 \sqrt{2} \\ & (-4+7 d) \hat{i}-\hat{j}(-2+42)+\hat{k}(d-12) \\ & (7 d-4)^2+(40)^2+(d-12)^2=1800 \\ & 50 d^2-80 d-40=0 \\ & 5 d^2-8 d-4=0 \\ & 5 d^2-10 d-2 d-4 \\ & 5 d(d-2)+2(d-2)=0 \\ & d=2 \text { or } d=-\frac{2}{5} \\ & \because d>0, d=2 \\ & (a+1) \hat{i}+(b+2) \hat{j}+(c-7) \hat{k}=6 \hat{i}+2 \hat{j}-2 \hat{k} \\ & a+1=6 \& b+2=2, c-7=-2 \\ & a=5 \quad b=0 \quad c=5 \\ & |A B|=\sqrt{1+4+49}=\sqrt{54} \\ & |B C|=\sqrt{25+25}=\sqrt{50} \\ & |A C|=\sqrt{86+4+4}=\sqrt{44} \end{aligned}$
Ans. 54
questions.source: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024

questions.q: If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mover><mi>a</mi><mo>→</mo></mover><mo>=</mo><mover><mi>i</mi><mo>^</mo></mover><mo>+</mo><mn>2</mn><mover><mi>j</mi><mo>^</mo></mover><mo>+</mo><mover><mi>k</mi><mo>^</mo></mover><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mover><mi>b</mi><mo>→</mo></mover><mo>=</mo><mn>3</mn><mfenced><mrow><mover><mi>i</mi><mo>^</mo></mover><mo>-</mo><mover><mi>j</mi><mo>^</mo></mover><mo>+</mo><mover><mi>k</mi><mo>^</mo></mover></mrow></mfenced></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mover><mi mathvariant="normal">c</mi><mo>→</mo></mover></math> be the vector such that <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mover><mi>a</mi><mo>→</mo></mover><mo>×</mo><mover><mi>c</mi><mo>→</mo></mover><mo>=</mo><mover><mi>b</mi><mo>→</mo></mover></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mover><mi>a</mi><mo>→</mo></mover><mo>·</mo><mover><mi>c</mi><mo>→</mo></mover><mo>=</mo><mn>3</mn></math>, then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mover><mi>a</mi><mo>→</mo></mover><mo>·</mo><mo>(</mo><mfenced><mrow><mover><mi>c</mi><mo>→</mo></mover><mo>×</mo><mover><mi>b</mi><mo>→</mo></mover></mrow></mfenced><mo>-</mo><mover><mi>b</mi><mo>→</mo></mover><mo>-</mo><mover><mi>c</mi><mo>→</mo></mover><mo>)</mo></math> is equal to

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mover><mi>a</mi><mo>→</mo></mover><mo>=</mo><mover><mi>i</mi><mo>^</mo></mover><mo>+</mo><mi>α</mi><mover><mi>j</mi><mo>^</mo></mover><mo>+</mo><mi>β</mi><mover><mi>k</mi><mo>^</mo></mover><mo> </mo><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi>α</mi><mo>,</mo><mi>β</mi><mo>∈</mo><mi>R</mi></math>. Let a vector <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mover><mi>b</mi><mo>→</mo></mover></math> be such that the angle between <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mover><mi>a</mi><mo>→</mo></mover></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mover><mi>b</mi><mo>→</mo></mover></math> is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><mi>π</mi><mn>4</mn></mfrac></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mfenced close="|" open="|"><mover><mi>b</mi><mo>→</mo></mover></mfenced><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>=</mo><mn>6</mn></math>, If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mover><mi>a</mi><mo>→</mo></mover><mo>·</mo><mover><mi>b</mi><mo>→</mo></mover><mo>=</mo><mn>3</mn><msqrt><mn>2</mn></msqrt></math>, then the value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mrow><msup><mi>α</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><msup><mi>β</mi><mn>2</mn></msup></mrow></mfenced><mo>|</mo><mover><mi>a</mi><mo>→</mo></mover><mo>×</mo><mover><mi>b</mi><mo>→</mo></mover><msup><mo>|</mo><mn>2</mn></msup></math> is equal to
questions.solution:
Given: b→2=6, a→·b→=32, a→=i^+αj^+βk^

We know that, a→.b→=a→b→cosθ

⇒32=12+α2+β26cosπ4

⇒32=12+α2+β23

⇒1+α2+β2=6

⇒1+α2+β2=6

⇒α2+β2=5

Also, a→×b→=a→b→sinθ

⇒a→×b→=66sinπ4=62

⇒a→×b→2=18

⇒α2+β2a→×b→2=5×18

⇒α2+β2a→×b→2=90
questions.source: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mover><mi>a</mi><mo>→</mo></mover></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mover><mi>b</mi><mo>→</mo></mover></math> be two vectors such that <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>|</mo><mover><mi>b</mi><mo>→</mo></mover><mo>|</mo><mo>=</mo><mn>1</mn></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>|</mo><mover><mi>b</mi><mo>→</mo></mover><mo>×</mo><mover><mi>a</mi><mo>→</mo></mover><mo>|</mo><mo>=</mo><mn>2</mn></math> Then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>|</mo><mo>(</mo><mover><mi>b</mi><mo>→</mo></mover><mo>×</mo><mover><mi>a</mi><mo>→</mo></mover><mo>)</mo><mo>-</mo><mover><mi>b</mi><mo>→</mo></mover><msup><mo>|</mo><mn>2</mn></msup></math> is equal to
questions.solution:
Given: b→=1, b→×a→=2

Now, solving b→×a→-b→2,

⇒b→×a→-b→2=b→×a→2+b→2-2b→×a→·b→

Now, we know that, b→×a→·b→=b→×a→·a→=0

⇒b→×a→-b→2=22+12-2×0

⇒b→×a→-b→2=5
questions.source: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024

questions.q: Let a→=3i^+2j^+k^,b→=2i^−j^+3k^ and c→ be a vector such that a→+b→×c→=2a→×b→+24j^−6k^ and a→−b→+i^.c→=−3. Then c→2 is equal to _______.
questions.solution:
Given,

a→=3i^+2j^+k^, b→=2i^−j^+3k^ and c→ be a vector such that a→+b→×c→=2a→×b→+24j^−6k^ 

Now, finding a→×b→=i^j^k^3212-13=7i^−7j^−7k^

Now, solving

a→+b→×c→=2a→×b→+24j^−6k^

⇒5i^+j^+4k^×c→=27i^−7j^−7k^+24j^−6k^

⇒i^j^k^514xyz=14i^+10j^−20k^

⇒i^z−4y−j^5z−4x+k^5y−x=14i^+10j^−20k^

Now, on comparing both side, 

z−4y=14, 4x−5z=10, 5y−x=−20  ....i

Now, solving 

a→−b→+i^·c→=−3

⇒2i^+3j^−2k^·c→=−3

⇒2x+3y−2z=−3 .......ii

Now, solving the equation i & ii we get,

∴ x=5, y=−3, z=2

⇒c→2=25+9+4=38
questions.source: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mover><mi>a</mi><mo>→</mo></mover><mo>=</mo><mover><mi>i</mi><mo>^</mo></mover><mo>+</mo><mover><mi>j</mi><mo>^</mo></mover><mo>+</mo><mover><mi>k</mi><mo>^</mo></mover><mo>,</mo><mover><mi>b</mi><mo>→</mo></mover><mo>=</mo><mo>−</mo><mover><mi>i</mi><mo>^</mo></mover><mo>−</mo><mn>8</mn><mover><mi>j</mi><mo>^</mo></mover><mo>+</mo><mn>2</mn><mover><mi>k</mi><mo>^</mo></mover></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mover><mi>c</mi><mo>→</mo></mover><mo>=</mo><mn>4</mn><mover><mi>i</mi><mo>^</mo></mover><mo>+</mo><msub><mi>c</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><mover><mi>j</mi><mo>^</mo></mover><mo>+</mo><msub><mi>c</mi><mn>3</mn></msub><mover><mi>k</mi><mo>^</mo></mover></math> be three vectors such that <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mover><mi>b</mi><mo>→</mo></mover><mo>×</mo><mover><mi>a</mi><mo>→</mo></mover><mo>=</mo><mover><mi>c</mi><mo>→</mo></mover><mo>×</mo><mover><mi>a</mi><mo>→</mo></mover></math>. If the angle between the vector <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mover><mi>c</mi><mo>→</mo></mover></math> and the vector <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>3</mn><mover><mi>i</mi><mo>^</mo></mover><mo>+</mo><mn>4</mn><mover><mi>j</mi><mo>^</mo></mover><mo>+</mo><mover><mi>k</mi><mo>^</mo></mover></math> is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>θ</mi></math>, then the greatest integer less than or equal to <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>tan</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mi>θ</mi></math> is:
questions.solution:
Given: a→=i^+j^+k, b→=−i^−8j^+2k^ and c→=4i^+c2j^+c3k

Also, b→×a→=c→×a→

⇒b→−c→×a→=0

⇒b→−c→=λa→

⇒b→=c→+λa→

⇒−i^-8j^+2k=4i^+c2j^+c3k+λi^+j^+k

⇒λ+4=−1, λ+c2=−8, λ+c3=2

⇒λ=−5, c2=−3, c3=7

⇒c→=4i^−3j^+7k

Now, finding angle we get,

cosθ=3i^+4j^+k^·4i^−3j^+7k^3i^+4j^+k^⋅4i^−3j^+7k^

⇒cosθ=12−12+726⋅74

⇒cosθ=726⋅74

⇒cosθ=72481

⇒secθ=24817

⇒sec2θ=4×48149

⇒1+tan2θ=192449

⇒tan2θ=187549

⇒tan2θ≈38.26

⇒tan2θ=38.26=38
questions.source: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
